optimizer_switches.name: hash_join
optimizer_switches.explanation: Builds an in-memory hash table on the smaller (build) input and probes it from the other side. Uses join_buffer_size; spills to a tmp file (and tmpdir) if the build side does not fit.

warnings.text: Full table scan: users (3000 rows), orders (12000 rows)
1 hash join(s) — uses join_buffer_size (~12000 rows in build phase)

suggestions.action: Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans
Increase join_buffer_size if hash joins are slow or spill to disk

executive_summary: Query scans 2 tables and hash-joins 1 pair; returns 12,000 rows in 8.2 ms. Main finding: hash join uses join_buffer_size; an index on the join column would be faster.

query.raw: /* select#1 */ select `u`.`name` AS `name`, `o`.`total` AS `total` from `testdb`.`users` `u` join `testdb`.`orders` `o` on (`o`.`user_id` = `u`.`id`)
query.beautified:
select u.name AS name, o.total AS total
from testdb.users u
  join testdb.orders o on (o.user_id = u.id)
